data.title: Stock index futures fall September CPI rises more than expected
data.description:
Stock index futures fell on Thursday, as September consumer inflation comes in hotter than expected.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.2%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was flat at 4.07%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.02%.

Major U.S. stock averages closed in the green on Wednesday, with the S&P 500 (SP500) and the Dow (DJI) ending at new peaks after the Federal Reserve's September meeting minutes said a "substantial majority" on its rate-setting board backed its big half-point cut to start an easing cycle.

"Markets have put in another strong performance over the last 24 hours, as optimism about the near-term economic outlook continues to build," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

"Reading the (FOMC) Minutes, CPI is important to what happens next," Economist Steven Blitz from Global Data TS Lombard said.

September Consumer Price Index +0.2% M/M vs. +0.1% expected and +0.2% prior, +2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.3% expected and +2.5% prior.

Core CPI, which excludes food and energy: +0.3% M/M vs. +0.2% expected and +0.3% prior, +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.2% expected and +3.2% prior.

The CPI report is in particular focus after the strong jobs report last Friday, as there’s been growing speculation that the Fed might not cut at all at their next meeting in November, Allen added.

The initial jobless claims climbed more than expected in the past week.

The Fed balance sheet will come later in the day.

data.title: YY Group regains compliance with minimum bid price rule
data.description:
• YY Group (NASDAQ:YYGH) said the company has regained compliance with the Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule.
  • Press Release

data.title: September CPI rises more than expected, indicating inflation risks remain
data.description:
• The progress of disinflation appeared to stall in September as the Consumer Price Index rose +0.2% M/M, stronger than the +0.1% expected and flat with August's +0.2% increase, according to U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics data released on Thursday.
  • Headline CPI increased 2.4% Y/Y vs. +2.3% expected and +2.5% prior.
  • Inflation in core CPI, which excludes food and energy, also stalled, rising +0.3% M/M vs. +0.2% expected and +0.3% prior.
  • +3.3% Y/Y vs. +3.2% expected and +3.2% prior.
  • Developing... check back for updates.

data.title: Arcadium Lithium downgraded at HSBC after Rio Tinto's $6.7B bid
data.description:
Arcadium Lithium (NYSE:ALTM) on Thursday was downgraded to Hold from a previous investment rating of Buy among analysts at financial-services firm HSBC. They said the lithium miner is fairly valued after its board approved a $6.7 billion all-cash acquisition offer by Rio Tinto (RIO).

Shares of Arcadium (ALTM) surged 31% to a nine-month high on Wednesday after the deal was announced.

“Though the sale agreement occurs at what we believe to be the trough of the cycle, the valuation seems fair given near-term risks to capacity and a possible equity dilution and/or leveraged balance sheet had the company raised additional financing,” Santhosh Seshadri, analyst at HSBC, said in an October 10 report.

The risks that the deal will fall through are minimal, according to the bank.

“We don’t see antitrust hurdles to deal completion as Rio Tinto (RIO) is not a lithium-producing company,” HSBC said. “Supermajority shareholder vote (75%) requires the blessings of Blackrock and Vanguard that cumulatively own a roughly 20% stake. The scope for counteroffer seems limited, given likely limited interest from most diversified miners.”

Lithium is a key ingredient in rechargeable batteries, including those for electric vehicles.

data.title: 'Out of touch:' Skyworks Solutions, Cirrus Logic sink on Barclays downgrade
data.description:
Skyworks Solutions (NASDAQ:SWKS) and Cirrus Logic (NASDAQ:CRUS) were in the spotlight on Thursday as Barclays downgraded the semiconductor companies, citing "out of touch" expectations for smartphones.

Skyworks fell 2.8% in premarket trading, while Cirrus Logic was down 1.8%.

"We are moderating unit assumptions in conjunction with our Hardware team, adding the [iPhone SE 4] with internal modem, adjusting [iPhone 17] content, and stepping to the sidelines on CRUS to [Equal-Weight], while moving SWKS to [Underweight]," analyst Tom O'Malley wrote in a note to clients.

In addition to the downgrades, O'Malley cut the price targets on Skyworks Solutions and Cirrus Logic to $87 and $120 from $115 and $140, respectively.

O'Malley added that content from Skyworks Solutions in the next iPhone should be "modestly down," while simultaneously increasing for Qorvo (QRVO).

Cirrus Logic should also see an added camera controller in the iPhone 17, but O'Malley said the story for Cirrus Logic has "largely played out until we have line of sight on content increases in the [iPhone 18]."

Analysts are cautious on Skyworks Solutions (SWKS). It has a HOLD rating from Seeking Alpha authors, while Wall Street analysts rate it a HOLD. Conversely, Seeking Alpha's quant system, which consistently beats the market, rates SWKS a HOLD.

data.title: Chili's turnaround drives upgrade to Brinker International
data.description:
Brinker International’s (NYSE:EAT) impressive rally this year (+92% vs +21% for the S&P 500) coupled with expectations for a turnaround at Chili’s led BofA Securities to upgrade the stock to Neutral from Underperform with the bank now seeing the risk/reward as balanced.

Specific to Chili’s (which represents 89% of EAT’s revenue) the operational improvements and more effective marketing put in place by CEO Kevin Hochman has repositioned the restaurant chain into a better company and better brand.

But idiosyncratic tailwinds such as elevated food prices and less marketing from competitors that have previously benefited Chili’s are unlikely to last, BofA’s Katherine Griffin says. And as Chili’s adjusts its prices more in line with its competitors, the move could serve as a disadvantage as price sensitivity lures consumers to lower-priced options.

“We view EAT’s outperformance as justified though risk/reward for the stock looks balanced from here,” Griffin says, adding that, “While EAT should continue to benefit in the near term from the catalysts that drove its turnaround – primarily better operations and increased advertising – we question the longer-term sustainability of topline growth that underpins higher valuation.”

In addition to an upgrade to Neutral, Griffin raises her target price for Brinker International (EAT) by 42% to $90.

Shares are up 0.8% in Thursday’s premarket.

data.title: Bellatora announces corporate name change to Kinetic Seas and ticker symbol change to "KSEZ"
data.description:
• Kinetic Seas has officially changed its corporate name to Kinetic Seas (OTCPK:KSEZ).
  • This name change reflects the Co.'s expanded focus on delivering cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions for businesses.
  • The Co.'s common stock, quoted on the OTC Pink marketplace under the former ticker symbol "ECGR" will continue to be quoted on the OTC Pink marketplace under the new ticker symbol KSEZ effective immediately.
  • "Our new name encapsulates the constant evolution of AI technology and our role in navigating businesses through this exciting, ever-changing landscape." said Edward Honour, CEO.

data.title: Atlassian keeps Buy rating at Canaccord
data.description:
Canaccord maintained its Buy rating on Atlassian (NASDAQ:TEAM) and raised the price target raised on the shares to $225 from $200.

The Australian company develops several software products, including a project management system called Jira, which connects technical and business teams.

The analysts said that their conversations with investors have been upbeat — the scope of the opportunity, the commitment to margin re-expansion, and the 'new' guidance philosophy all stand out. In relation to guidance, the fiscal first quarter results will be a good test with respect to just how conservatively recast Cloud growth estimates are for the fiscal year 2025.

The analysts think there could be a few points of upside to Atlassian's fiscal first quarter guidance of 27% Cloud growth, which they think could serve as an important first step in unlocking a gradual re-rating of the company's stock higher.

Atlassian (TEAM) has a Hold rating at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating system, which consistently beats the market. Meanwhile, the Seeking Alpha authors' average rating is more positive with a Buy, and so is the average Wall Street analysts' rating, Buy.

data.title: bioAffinity Technologies names J. Michael Edwards as CFO
data.description:
• bioAffinity Technologies (BIAFW) Thursday said that J. Michael Edwards has accepted the position of Chief Financial Officer.
  • Edwards has been bioAffinity’s interim CFO since Sept. 15 and previously served as bioAffinity’s consulting CFO from 2014 to 2023, overseeing the company’s initial public offering in 2022.
  • Edwards will report directly to bioAffinity Technologies (BIAFW) President and CEO Maria Zannes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: United Airlines sets itself apart with aggressive international expansion plans
data.description:
United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) announced on Thursday the largest international expansion in its history, with service to eight new cities.

Starting in May 2025, the carrier will launch five new nonstop flights from its hub in Newark/New York to destinations no other U.S. airline. The new destinations include Nuuk, Greenland; Palermo, Italy; Bilbao, Spain; Madeira Island, Portugal and Faro, Portugal. The airline is also adding three new nonstop routes from Washington D.C./Dulles, including its first-ever flight to Dakar, Senegal operating year-round and new seasonal nonstop flights to Nice, France and Venice, Italy.

Notably, United Airlines (UAL) is already the U.S. carrier with the most flights across the Atlantic. Looking ahead, the summer 2025 transatlantic schedule will be the largest in the company's history, with more than 760 weekly flights. In addition to new flights, customers will have even more time to explore Venice, Italy; Athens, Greece; and Barcelona, Spain with earlier start dates next summer. The airline will also increase the number of weekly direct flights between Newark/New York-Palma de Mallorca, Spain, Newark/New York-Dubrovnik, Croatia, and Newark/New York-Athens, Greece. United (UAL) is also adding new direct flights from Tokyo-Narita to Ulaanbaatar, Mongolia and Kaohsiung – destinations no other U.S. airline serves – and a new nonstop flight to Koror, Palau. Those new flights will connect in Tokyo to United's (UAL) transpacific services to five hub locations in the continental United States. In total, next summer, United will offer 800 daily flights to and from 147 international destinations – including 40 that no other U.S. airline serves.

Shares of United Airlines (UAL) fell 2.40% in premarket action following soft guidance from rival Delta Air Lines (DAL).
